title: Elasticity
subject: Economics
objectives: Calculate elasticity using the midpoint formula
Distinguish elastic, inelastic, and unit elastic demand

sections.heading: What is elasticity?
sections.content:
Elasticity measures how responsive quantity demanded is to a change in price. If a small price increase causes customers to stop buying, demand is elastic. If a price increase barely changes buying behavior, demand is inelastic.

Think of it this way: if the price of a specific brand of bottled water doubles, you would just buy a different brand (elastic). But if the price of insulin doubles, diabetic patients still need it (inelastic).

sections.heading: Factors affecting elasticity
sections.content:
Demand tends to be more elastic when:
• Close substitutes exist (Coke vs. Pepsi)
• The good is a luxury rather than a necessity
• The good takes a large share of the buyer's budget
• Consumers have more time to adjust their behavior

Demand tends to be more inelastic when:
• Few or no substitutes exist (gasoline, prescription drugs)
• The good is a necessity
• The good is a small fraction of income (salt, paper clips)

sections.heading: Calculating elasticity
sections.content:
Price elasticity of demand = % change in quantity demanded ÷ % change in price

If the price of movie tickets rises from $10 to $12 (20% increase) and ticket sales fall from 1000 to 800 (20% decrease):

Elasticity = 20% ÷ 20% = 1.0 (unit elastic)

Interpretation:
• |E| > 1: Elastic — quantity changes more than price
• |E| = 1: Unit elastic — quantity changes proportionally
• |E| < 1: Inelastic — quantity changes less than price

Elasticity measures how sensitive demand is to price changes. Goods with close substitutes tend to be elastic; necessities without substitutes tend to be inelastic. This concept is crucial for business pricing decisions.

aiContext: This lesson covers price elasticity of demand at a high school microeconomics level. Students learn what elasticity measures, the factors that affect it, and how to calculate and interpret elasticity values.
